articles.title: Weekly Numerology Horoscope (1st June - 7th June, 2026): A Powerful Week of Growth, Confidence & New Opportunities
articles.description: Week 23 of 2026 arrives with a fresh and dynamic vibration that pushes people toward action, confidence, and practical movement. After weeks of emotional healing and inner reflection, the energy now becomes faster, clearer, and more goal-oriented. This week carries strong vibrations of new beginnings, leadership, career focus, financial planning, and rebuilding personal motivation. Many people may suddenly feel more energetic, ambitious, and mentally active. Situations that were delayed earlier may begin moving quickly now. The universe is encouraging you to stop doubting yourself and start taking practical steps toward your future.

articles.title: When Sunil Dutt revealed why he sought Bal Thackeray’s help during Sanjay Dutt’s 1993 case and his friendship with Haji Mastan
articles.description: As Sanjay Dutt remains one of Bollywood’s most talked-about stars, conversations around the turbulent phase of his life during the 1993 Mumbai blasts case continue to resurface even today. One such moment was veteran actor and politician Sunil Dutt candidly opening up about seeking help from Shiv Sena supremo Bal Thackeray when his son was arrested under TADA and the Arms Act.

articles.description: According to the complainant, Jyoti Devi, a resident of Balaji Colony on Sahabana Road, Aashika had left home with her friend to purchase a birthday cake

articles.title: Spanish Proverb of the Day: "Tell me who you hang out with, and I'll tell you who..." Want to know your future? Look at the people around you — this timeless Spanish proverb reveals why your circle shapes your character, choices, and destiny
articles.description: Spanish Proverb of the Day: “Tell me who you hang out with, and I’ll tell you who...” reveals a timeless truth about human behavior, friendship, influence, and personal growth. This powerful Spanish proverb explains how social circles shape mindset, habits, success, and emotional health. People slowly absorb the energy around them. Wise company builds character. Toxic company destroys peace. In today’s fast-moving world, this deep life lesson feels more relevant, transformational, and emotionally important than ever.

articles.title: Pune Hooch tragedy kills 15; 8 arrested
articles.description: At least 15 people have died and several others hospitalized after allegedly consuming suspected spurious liquor in Pune and Pimpri-Chinchwad. Police have arrested eight individuals, including the alleged bootlegger, and are investigating the illicit liquor network. The Chief Minister has ordered a detailed inquiry and assured strict action.

articles.title: Telangana far ahead of other states in paddy procurement: Uttam
articles.description: Telangana ramps up paddy procurement to 60 LMT as FCI central procurement declines; Uttam Kumar Reddy urges higher Centre allocations; Rs 11,050 crore paid.

articles.title: VVCE hosts FUSION X 1.0 hackathon
articles.description: The Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at Vidyavardhaka College of Engineering (VVCE), Mysuru, in collaboration with Inunity, successfully organised FUSION X 1.0, a 24-hour national-level Hackathon, on the college premises here on Friday.

articles.title: Johnny Depp allegedly loses nearly Rs 7 crore in credit card fraud; Hungarian police arrest suspect

articles.title: Supreme Court Pulls Up NTA Over NEET 2026 Leak | Centre Says PM Modi Supervising Reforms
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Friday sharply questioned the Centre and the National Testing Agency over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak controversy, raising concerns about accountability, institutional failures and the trauma caused to students and families. During the hearing, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta informed the bench that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is personally monitoring the re-examination and reform process to ensure there are no further lapses. The Centre told the Court that new security mechanisms have been introduced ahead of the rescheduled NEET-UG exam on June 21. The NTA informed the Court that sweeping structural reforms have been implemented, including mandatory CCTV checks, forensic analysis of surveillance footage, mock drills, emergency preparedness plans and strengthened coordination with police and intelligence agencies. The Court questioned how the leak occurred despite regular monitoring by the High Powered Committee and observed that accountability within the NTA remained unclear. Stressing the need for lasting institutional reforms, the bench directed the Ministry of Education to file a separate affidavit explaining how the NEET examination process would be institutionalised on a year-to-year basis to prevent future controversies. The matter will now be heard again in July.

articles.title: Sebi eases nomination rules for demat accounts, mutual funds. Check full details
articles.description: SEBI has relaxed nomination norms for demat accounts and mutual funds starting September 1, 2026. Nomination is mandatory for single holders unless opted out, but optional for joint accounts. The process is simplified, reducing documentation and enabling digital submissions.

articles.title: 'No Need To Sing Vande Matram In...' Pinarayi Vijayan Hits out at Centre, BJP After Row In Assembly
articles.description: Kerala opposition leader Pinarayi Vijayan has criticised the Centre and BJP over the Vande Mataram controversy in the state assembly. He alleged full rendition of the national song is part of RSS agenda and defended partial performance. BJP accused the assembly of insulting the national song during Governor Arlekar’s address. CPM and BJP exchanged sharp political attacks over protocol, secularism and historical usage. The state government clarified it had no role, saying the programme was organised by Lok Bhavan authorities.

articles.title: Kapil Dev interview on life, cricket and golf | IPL
Global air passenger demand falls 3.4% amid West Asia crisis: IATA
articles.description: Global air passenger demand dropped by 3.4% in April 2026, dragged down by a 46.6% fall for Middle East carriers due to regional tensions, reports IATA. Excluding the Middle East, demand saw a modest increase of 1.2% amid volatile conditions.

articles.title: Jairam Ramesh hails Siddaramaiah as influential leader in Karnataka

articles.description: A three-day Tibetan cultural festival commenced in Dharamshala, showcasing Tibetan culture, history and traditions. Inaugurated by Sikyong Penpa Tsering, the event aims to strengthen engagement with the Indian community and raise awareness.

articles.title: Kharge meets outgoing Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah, praises his service

articles.title: Dowry Death Case: Ex-judge Giribala, son sent to 5-day CBI remand
articles.description: A Bhopal court has sent former judge Giribala Singh and her son Samarth Singh to a five-day CBI remand in the alleged dowry death case of Twisha Sharma. The CBI will now interrogate both accused as part of its ongoing investigation.

'Priyasakhi' is sung by K S Harisankar and music of the song is given by Hesham Abdul Wahab. Lyrics of 'Epic: First Semester' song 'Priyasakhi' are written by Suresh Banisetti.
